item.title: More than double the gas stuck in Hormuz is wasted each year, IEA says
item.description: Capping methane emissions would alleviate energy and climate crisis

item.title: FirstFT: US to ‘guide’ ships out of Hormuz, says Trump
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Airlines slash 2mn seats and EU urged to adapt to economic shifts

item.title: UAE fertiliser giant resorts to trucks to shift product out of Gulf
item.description: Abu Dhabi’s Fertiglobe switches to land cargo to avoid Strait of Hormuz as soaring prices allow it to shoulder extra costs

item.title: Greg Abel tells Berkshire holders, be patient
item.description: Plus, Elliott likens Iran war to battling Nazis, Wall Street piles into Treasuries, and Raphael at The Met

item.title: Airlines slash flights as fuel shortage fears mount
item.description: Millions of seats cut as Middle East crisis throws global travel into disarray

item.title: UK pub groups betting on World Cup to counter sluggish consumer demand
item.description: Optimism comes as a result of early bookings despite the sector enduring tepid sales growth in March

item.title: How the UK’s ‘King of Trainers’ ran into trouble
item.description: The athleisure boom turned JD Sports into a global powerhouse but slowing sales have led to conflict in the boardroom

item.title: The law firm that shaped Wall Street and the world
item.description: With fewer than 300 lawyers, Wachtell racked up bumper profits and exercised outsize influence. Now its model is being challenged as never before

item.title: Heat pump sales jump as consumers recoil at high fossil fuel prices
item.description: High demand for units in Europe suggests green technologies benefiting from energy price surge due to Iran war

item.title: ‘It’s crucial’: how AI is reshaping the fragrance industry
item.description: From hyper-personalisation to cutting costs, artificial intelligence is getting a hold on your perfume cabinet

item.title: Lessons from the agentic AI trailblazers
item.description: Many businesses have yet to deploy agents, so what tips can early adopters offer?

item.title: The land of milk and no money: UK farmers are in a fix
item.description: Prices are falling due to a glut of the white stuff, yet the costs of feed, fertilisers and fuel are rising

item.title: Rise in US political violence fuels demand for active-shooter insurance
item.description: Brokers report growing interest from ‘almost every sector’ as companies reassess security risks

item.title: Water utilities jettison listening sticks and embrace AI
item.description: World leaders in the sector, such as Singapore, have leakage rates that are 75% lower than in England and Wales

item.title: Saudi Arabia touts new use for Neom port after Iran war
item.description: With the Strait of Hormuz choked off, Riyadh focuses on Red Sea infrastructure

item.title: Wealth managers insist AI can work in their favour
item.description: Fears about the technology’s impact hit share prices but sector is embracing the benefits

item.title: Hedge funds seek an edge by using AI’s speed
item.description: Investors are using the technology to analyse documents but are holding it back from more sensitive tasks

item.title: Recruiters turn to AI in quest to find the perfect connection
item.description: Technology is being used to ‘clear the decks for human moments’

item.title: Start-ups move fast with AI-generated code
item.description: Founders are overcoming longstanding bottlenecks in product development

item.title: Restaurants lean on AI to cut waste and reduce costs
item.description: Hospitality sector has been slower than others at adopting the technology

item.title: Global industries squeezed as Iran war enters third month
item.description: Middle East crisis throws travel and commodities into disarray

item.title: GameStop makes unsolicited $56bn offer for eBay
item.description: Video game retailer offers $125 a share for resale web giant

item.title: Greg Abel leaves unanswered questions about Berkshire’s war chest
item.description: Investors have been given few clues on the strategy of Warren Buffett’s successor

item.title: Dubai food conglomerate IFFCO set to go into provisional liquidation
item.description: Creditors nominated FTI Consulting in effort to protect assets as firm struggles with $2bn debt pile and fallout from Iran war

item.title: British local elections set to be crucial moment for Starmer
item.description: Votes in England, Scotland and Wales, New York hosts the Met Gala and broadcaster David Attenborough turns 100

item.title: Banks seek to offload risk to avoid ‘choking’ on data centre debt
item.description: Global lenders explore private deals and risk transfers to cut exposure to AI boom

item.title: US pullback on long-range missiles leaves Europe exposed
item.description: Cancelled deployment of weapons battalion as continent is re-arming threatens Nato deterrence against Russia

item.title: Trump administration cites national security to widen clampdown on wind farms
item.description: Defence department is stalling 165 projects as president steps up efforts to stamp out the industry

item.title: Trump’s green new deal
item.description: The US president’s actions have inadvertently raised the appeal of renewable energy

item.title: US blocking tactics delay global shipping carbon levy talks
item.description: Legal adoption of deal agreed by majority a year ago pushed into limbo
